candidate_problems.text: Under what task conditions can a smaller model with conjecture-criticism architecture outperform a much larger model lacking that structure on genuine explanation-generation rather than on proxy metrics?
How much substrate capacity is minimally required before architectural search and criticism can operate over structured explanatory objects rather than collapsing into noise?
Are there domains where scale itself effectively implements the needed search compression, making the architecture-vs-scale distinction weaker than the conjecture claims?
Which feedback-loop designs for memory, criticism, and promotion actually produce cumulative epistemic progress instead of just better local selection among generated outputs?
